details.body:
finding_summary: The new beads health guard only runs inside `handle_propose_new_bead`; the normal bead-closing and bead-claiming flows still mutate `.beads` without any preflight validation.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2998-3029
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:204-216
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:377-430
- src/cli/project.rs:964-981
remediation_hint: Move the health check into a shared helper and invoke it before every milestone mutation entrypoint that updates or closes bead state, not just before proposed-bead creation, so conflicted or missing `.beads/issues.jsonl` stops the operation before `br update`/`br close`/`br sync` runs.

finding_summary: `check_beads_health()` misidentifies ordinary `issues.jsonl` content as a merge conflict because it uses raw substring checks for `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, and `>>>>>>>`, and it also collapses every non-`NotFound` read failure into `ConflictMarkers`.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:25-31
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:45-46
remediation_hint: Detect real conflict-marker lines instead of any substring match, and preserve other read failures as distinct errors so operators are not told to resolve a nonexistent merge conflict.

Bug in br_health.rs:31 — the catch-all `Err(_) => return BeadsHealthStatus::ConflictMarkers` misclassifies non-NotFound I/O errors (permission denied, disk errors) as conflict markers. The downstream error message in service.rs:3004 then tells the operator to "resolve the conflict" when the real problem is unreadable file state. Fix: either add a new `BeadsHealthStatus::IoError` variant, or fall through to `BrUnavailable` which at least doesn't produce an actively wrong diagnosis. The fail-safe blocking behavior is correct, only the reported reason is wrong.

finding_summary: `BrMutationAdapter::sync_import()` only checks the adapter's in-memory dirty flag, so every fresh adapter instance is treated as clean even after a prior process crashed with unflushed `br` state. `BrMutationAdapter::new()` / `with_adapter()` always initialize `has_unsync_mutations` to `false` (src/adapters/br_process.rs:496-516), and `sync_import()` relies exclusively on `ensure_synced()` before running `br sync --import-only` (src/adapters/br_process.rs:529-543, 713-724). That means the documented recovery flow after `git pull` / failed-run restart can still import upstream JSONL over stale local mutations, which defeats the safety guarantee this bead is adding. severity: high. affected_files: src/adapters/br_process.rs. remediation_hint: persist or reconstruct pending-mutation state across restarts before allowing `sync_import()`, or narrow the API so import is rejected unless the caller has independently proven the workspace is clean.

Missing test for `BrMutationAdapter::sync_import()` refusing to run when the adapter has pending (dirty) mutations. The doc comments and `ensure_synced` guard in `sync_import()` codify this as a key safety invariant, but no test exercises this rejection path. Add a test in `src/adapters/br_process.rs` that: (1) makes a mutation to set the dirty flag, (2) calls `sync_import()`, (3) asserts the call returns an error about pending mutations, and (4) verifies no subprocess was actually invoked via the command log.

finding_summary: The new pending-mutation marker is written inside `.beads/` but is not ignored, so the repo's standard `git add .beads/` workflow can stage and commit transient dirty-state into source control. severity: high. affected_files: `src/adapters/br_process.rs`, `.gitignore`. remediation_hint: Move the marker outside `.beads/` or add an explicit ignore rule and regression test so `git add .beads/` cannot pick it up. evidence: `src/adapters/br_process.rs:26-28` defines `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations`, and `src/adapters/br_process.rs:567-576` writes it for every tracked mutation. `.gitignore:24-47` ignores other transient runtime artifacts but has no rule for this file. Once committed, other checkouts/restarts will falsely recover a dirty workspace and block import safety flows until a manual flush clears it.

finding_summary: `BrAdapter::check_available()` treats any successful spawn of `br --version` as healthy, even when the command exits non-zero, so the new health gate can report `Healthy` when `br` is actually unusable. severity: medium. affected_files: `src/adapters/br_process.rs`, `src/adapters/br_health.rs`. remediation_hint: Require `status.success()` and convert non-zero exits into `BrUnavailable` (or a structured `BrExitError`) so callers get the intended early failure. evidence: `src/adapters/br_process.rs:380-396` ignores the returned `ExitStatus` entirely and returns `Ok(())` after any completed process. `src/adapters/br_health.rs:18-19` and `src/adapters/br_health.rs:66-68` rely on that result to distinguish `Healthy` from `BrUnavailable`, so a broken `br` binary named `br` still passes preflight and only fails later during real mutations.

Bug in `exec_tracked_mutation` (src/adapters/br_process.rs:643): the `?` on `self.clear_pending_mutation_marker()?` in the `Err` branch will early-return with a marker-cleanup IO error, masking the original mutation error stored in `result`. When a mutation fails AND the adapter was not already dirty AND marker cleanup fails (e.g. disk full, permissions), the caller receives an IO error about file removal instead of the actual `BrExitError`/`BrTimeout`/etc. Fix: replace `self.clear_pending_mutation_marker()?` with `if let Err(e) = self.clear_pending_mutation_marker() { tracing::warn!(..., "failed to clear pending mutation marker after failed mutation: {e}"); }` so the original mutation error is always propagated.

finding_summary: The new `.br-unsynced-mutations` marker is not serialized with mutation/sync execution, so concurrent callers can delete it while unsynced work still exists.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs
file_refs:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:627-653
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:755-765
remediation_hint: Serialize `exec_tracked_mutation` and `sync_flush` behind a mutex or generation-based guard so only the operation that actually drains the last pending mutation can clear the on-disk marker.

finding_summary: `sync_import()` does not run the new beads export health check, so import-only sync still executes against missing or conflict-marked `.beads/issues.jsonl`.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs
- src/adapters/br_health.rs
file_refs:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:815-846
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:17-70
remediation_hint: Reuse `check_beads_health()` (or a shared lower-level guard) inside `sync_import()` and fail before spawning `br` when the export is missing, unreadable, or contains merge markers.

finding_summary: `check_beads_health()` never validates that `.beads/issues.jsonl` is parseable JSONL, so truncated or otherwise malformed exports are classified as `Healthy` and the new mutation guards still allow `br update/create/close` to run against corrupted bead state. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_health.rs", "src/cli/project.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs", "src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs"]. evidence: `src/adapters/br_health.rs:44-70` only checks file readability, conflict markers, and `br` availability; the new guard sites in `src/cli/project.rs:965-974`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2998-3020`, and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:117-123,221-223` trust that result before mutating beads. remediation_hint: treat malformed JSONL as unhealthy by parsing each line (or performing an equivalent bounded `br`-backed validation) before returning `Healthy`.

finding_summary: the new health-check path can hang bead mutations indefinitely because `check_beads_health()` calls `BrAdapter::check_available()`, and that probe uses blocking `std::process::Command::output()` with no timeout. severity: medium. affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_health.rs", "src/adapters/br_process.rs"]. evidence: `src/adapters/br_health.rs:18-20,91-95` invokes `BrAdapter::new().check_available()` on every health check, and `src/adapters/br_process.rs:386-389` runs `br --version` via `output()` instead of the timeout-governed process runner used elsewhere. remediation_hint: run the availability probe through the existing timed process path (or cache a startup check) so a wedged `br` binary cannot stall claim/close/create flows forever.

finding_summary: Retrying a bead claim after `br update --status=in_progress` succeeds but `br sync --flush-only` fails will replay `update` instead of flushing the recovered pending mutation marker. `BrMutationAdapter::with_adapter()` restores dirty state from `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations` ([src/adapters/br_process.rs:572-579]) and `sync_if_dirty()` exists for this exact replay path ([src/adapters/br_process.rs:870-881]), but `claim_bead_in_br()` still unconditionally calls `update_bead_status()` before `sync_flush()` ([src/cli/project.rs:972-988]). If `br` treats setting `in_progress` twice as an error, a transient sync failure becomes a hard claim failure on retry instead of replaying the already-successful local mutation.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs:972-988
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:572-579
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:870-881
remediation_hint: When a freshly constructed adapter comes up dirty, flush first with `sync_if_dirty()` (or otherwise honor the recovered pending-mutation marker) before issuing another `update_bead_status()` call.

finding_summary: The new JSONL health check only verifies that each non-empty line parses as any JSON value, so semantically broken exports such as `[]`, `42`, or a quoted string are treated as `Healthy`. All of the new mutation gates rely on this helper, so they will still mutate `.beads` on a corrupted export that is not actually newline-delimited issue records ([src/adapters/br_health.rs:94-109]). That leaves a real malformed-JSONL hole in the safety layer this bead is adding.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_health.rs:94-109
remediation_hint: Tighten `validate_jsonl_records()` so each non-empty line must deserialize to an object with the fields an issue export line is required to carry (at minimum `id`), and add a regression test for array/scalar lines.

finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br` can report success for a bead we never actually claimed
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/cli/project.rs
- src/adapters/br_process.rs
remediation_hint: Do not treat a generic recovered pending-mutation flush as proof that this bead's earlier `br update --status=in_progress` succeeded. Either persist bead/operation metadata with the marker and verify it matches `bead_id`, or always retry the explicit claim after a recovered flush unless you can prove the pending mutation came from this same claim.

evidence:
- `src/cli/project.rs:973-989` flushes any recovered `.br-unsynced-mutations` marker and immediately returns `Ok(())` whenever `br show` reports the target bead as `in_progress`.
- `src/adapters/br_process.rs:32-37` and `src/adapters/br_process.rs:698-706` make that marker repo-global for any successful mutation, not specifically for bead-claim updates.
- That means an unrelated unsynced mutation plus another operator winning the claim race can now be misclassified as "our earlier claim succeeded", which is exactly the race the surrounding comment at `src/cli/project.rs:960-964` says must not be treated as idempotent.

finding_summary: The new pending-mutation safety signal is only snapshotted once per adapter, so `sync_import()` and `sync_if_dirty()` can miss or erase unsynced state from other adapters/processes
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs
remediation_hint: Re-read and coordinate the on-disk dirty state under a repo-wide lock before `ensure_synced`, `sync_if_dirty`, and marker clearing, or replace the single shared marker with a multi-producer scheme that cannot be lost when another adapter flushes.

evidence:
- `src/adapters/br_process.rs:572-577` reads `.beads/.br-unsynced-mutations` only once into `has_unsync_mutations` during construction.
- `src/adapters/br_process.rs:648-652` and `src/adapters/br_process.rs:870-881` then decide whether import/flush is safe using only that cached boolean.
- `src/adapters/br_process.rs:549-552` shows synchronization is only per-adapter (`operation_lock`), while `src/adapters/br_process.rs:838-840` unconditionally clears the single repo-wide marker on any successful flush.
- In a multi-adapter or multi-process repo, one actor can therefore create or clear the marker without another long-lived adapter noticing, defeating the advertised crash-safe import guard and allowing pending local mutations to be missed after a crash/restart.

finding_summary: `BrMutationAdapter::with_adapter()` now creates `.beads/.br-sync.lock` as a side effect, but `.gitignore` never excludes that file, so the repository's documented `git add .beads/` workflow will stage a transient lock artifact.
severity: medium
affected_files: [".gitignore", "src/adapters/br_process.rs"]
remediation_hint: Add `.beads/.br-sync.lock` to `.gitignore` and extend the existing ignore regression test to cover the new lock file.
evidence:
- `REPO_OPERATION_LOCK` points at `.beads/.br-sync.lock` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:49-51`).
- `with_adapter()` eagerly calls `recover_pending_mutation_state()` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:688-690`), and that helper opens the lock file with `.create(true)` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:782-801`).
- `.gitignore` only excludes `.br-unsynced-mutations*`, not the new lock file (`.gitignore:24-26`).

finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br()` now treats `sync_if_dirty()` journal metadata as proof that a recovered claim succeeded, but that metadata neither proves the bead's final effective status nor covers legacy `.br-unsynced-mutations` recovery, so the claim path can return the wrong answer after a recovered flush.
severity: high
affected_files: ["src/adapters/br_process.rs", "src/cli/project.rs"]
remediation_hint: After a recovered flush, verify the bead's actual post-flush status again (as the removed `br show` fallback did) unless the recovery record can prove the final state unambiguously; keep a fallback path for legacy-marker recovery.
evidence:
- `sync_if_dirty()` only populates `matching_update_status` from JSON journal records and ignores `legacy_marker_present` when reporting what was replayed (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1233-1252`).
- `claim_bead_in_br()` returns immediately when any matching `update_bead_status(..., in_progress)` record was present, without checking the bead's actual status after the flush (`src/cli/project.rs:974-981`).

finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br` now treats any recovered flush plus `br show` reporting `in_progress` as proof that our earlier claim succeeded. At `src/cli/project.rs:979-989`, the new short-circuit no longer checks whether the recovered flush actually contained `update_status(bead_id, in_progress)`. If the repo merely had unrelated pending mutations and another operator already claimed this bead, this path returns `Ok(())`, so Ralph proceeds to create a project and keep the controller in a claimed state for a bead we do not own.
severity: high
affected_files: ["src/cli/project.rs"]
remediation_hint: Only skip the explicit `br update` when `sync_if_dirty()` proves the recovered flush included this bead's `update_status(..., in_progress)` record; otherwise fall through to a real claim attempt and surface the collision.

finding_summary: `handle_propose_new_bead` health-gates the entire function before any of its idempotent recovery branches. The upfront check at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3020` runs before the read-only reuse/reclassification paths at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3023-3159`, which can return an already-created bead or record a planned-elsewhere mapping without issuing any new `br` mutation. On replay after a previous successful create, a conflicted or temporarily missing `.beads/issues.jsonl` now causes the whole amendment to fail instead of reusing the existing result.
severity: medium
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs"]
remediation_hint: Move the beads-health guard down to the branches that are actually about to call `BrMutationAdapter` mutation methods (`add_dependency`, `create_bead`, `sync_flush`), so non-mutating recovery/bookkeeping paths remain idempotent.

finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br` can mistake another adapter's recovered `update --status=in_progress` as proof that the current caller already owns the bead.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:191-202
- src/adapters/br_process.rs:1233-1243
- src/cli/project.rs:973-989
remediation_hint: `sync_if_dirty()` currently reports every recovered `update_bead_status` record without preserving ownership, and `claim_bead_in_br()` returns success if the flushed repo now shows the target bead as `in_progress`. That means a second operator/process can flush someone else's failed-sync claim and then silently adopt it, creating a Ralph task without ever winning the claim itself. Only skip the explicit `br update` when you can match a stable claim identity, or always retry `br update` after a recovered flush and let br surface collisions.

finding_summary: The new beads-health guard is only enforced for the initial close path; later `comment_bead` mutations in success reconciliation still write to `.beads/` without rechecking health.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:222
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:875-917
remediation_hint: Re-run `ensure_beads_mutation_health` (or an equivalent guard) immediately before posting planned-elsewhere comments and before the follow-up `sync_flush`, and skip the non-blocking comment path when `.beads/issues.jsonl` is missing/conflicted/malformed.

finding_summary: The new safety gate is only checked once at the start of multi-step mutation flows, so later bead mutations can still run after `.beads/issues.jsonl` becomes conflicted or malformed. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3178-3217`, `ensure_beads_mutation_health()` runs before `create_bead`, but the follow-up `add_dependency` and `sync_flush` execute without another check. In `src/cli/project.rs:965-1000`, the same pattern exists around `sync_if_dirty()`: the explicit `update --status=in_progress` and final `sync_flush` are not revalidated after the recovered flush. A concurrent `git pull`/merge between those steps defeats the intended "pre-operation safety checks" and still allows later mutations on corrupted bead state.

finding_summary: `claim_bead_in_br` throws away the new `SyncIfDirtyOutcome` signal and always issues a second `br update --status=in_progress` after flushing recovered local mutations. In `src/cli/project.rs:969-992`, the retry path logs `recovered_flush.flushed_mutations()` but never checks whether the flushed journal already contained `update_bead_status(bead_id, "in_progress")`; `src/adapters/br_process.rs:1194-1253` added exactly that metadata. This means a retry after a successful local claim plus failed `sync --flush-only` still depends on `br update` being idempotent for an already-in-progress bead. The new tests even have to stub a backend that tolerates that second update. If real `br` rejects the no-op update, the recovered-claim retry path will keep failing even though the original claim was already flushed.

finding_summary: success reconciliation only health-checks `.beads/issues.jsonl` before `br close`, not before the mandatory `br sync --flush-only` that follows it. `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:226-239` now calls `ensure_beads_mutation_health(...)` before `close_bead_idempotent(...)`, but `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:440-454` still runs `sync_after_close()` unconditionally with no second health gate. If the export becomes conflicted, malformed, or missing after the local close but before the flush, this path bypasses the new operator-facing safety error and falls back to whatever raw `br sync` does. The claim and propose-new-bead paths already re-check health before their final flushes; reconciliation should do the same for the close-and-sync sequence.
